title: Relocating a Traditional Coffee Roastery (Buna): Handling Commercial Roasters and Fragile Glassware
excerpt: Traditional coffee roastery Buna relocation: commercial roasters, fragile glassware, jebena inventory, cafe equipment, Ethiopian coffee business move Addis Ababa.
content:
Relocating a traditional coffee roastery (buna): handling commercial roasters and fragile glassware

A buna roastery move smells like destiny and breaks like glass—commercial drum roasters weigh like small cars, jebena shelves sing fragility, and green bean inventory needs ventilation not suffocation. Traditional coffee businesses relocating must balance heavy machine rigging with delicate ceremony ware that brand identity depends on.

This guide handles commercial roasters and fragile glassware for traditional coffee roastery relocations.

Equipment inventory tiers

  • Roaster and afterburner: Rigging, disconnect gas/electric per vendor.
  • Grinders commercial: Lock burrs; upright transport.
  • Green bean storage: Breathable sacks; no airtight plastic long haul—mold risk.
  • Retail glassware: Jebena, cups, serving trays—cell dividers.
  • Ceremony furniture: Low tables, stools—pad legs.

Roaster relocation protocol

Manufacturer or certified technician disconnect and prep transport bolts. Crane or forklift at load if drum roaster—survey doorway and truck ramp. Gas line capped by licensed plumber; inspection before reconnect at new site. Never lay roaster on side without manual approval.

Fragile glassware packing

Each jebena wrapped; nested only with interleaf; upright carton with foam collar. Cup sets in dish packs; no rattling. Label CEREMONY GLASS—load mid-truck not under grinder.

Bean and retail inventory

Roasted beans sealed bags upright; avoid heat stack near truck roof. Retail packaging crush-sensitive—carton limit weight. POS and scale calibrated after move.

Regulatory and hygiene

Food business license address update; health inspection timing at new premises. Deep clean roaster duct before recommission—move dust contaminates flavor profile.

Customer continuity

Pop-up service or partner cafe during gap if loyal clientele daily. Signage "moving week" with new address—community follows buna loyalty.

Timing

Roaster cooled 24 hours before move—fire risk and burn injury. Early week load; reconnect and test roast small batch before grand reopening.

Active Movers roastery tier

Disclose roaster weight and gas—rigging line item in commercial annex.

Conclusion

Roastery relocation pairs rigging discipline for commercial heat machines with ceremony-grade glass packing—buna business reopens when roast drum turns and jebena survives same truck.
author: Active Movers Team
